content.full_text:
DOBBS & NIELSEN 
SUITE 2500, THE ALCOA BUILDING 
ONE MARITIME PLAZA 
SAN FRANC1SCO, CA 94111 
TELEPHONE (415) 362"1940 
July 26, 1979 
Ms. Regina Siciliano Kutchins 
Fair Political Practices commission 
Technical Assistance Division 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
Dear Ms. Kutchins: 
This will confirm our telephone conversation 
on July 26, 1979 regarding the filing of appropriate 
campaign statements by a public official who is being 
recalled. 
I explained that a recall has been instituted 
against the public official. 
A separate committee has 
been established to oppose the recall effort which is 
independent of the official being recalled. 
It is clear that the anti-recall committee, 
acting for the benefit of the official being recalled, 
must file its campaign reports 35 days after the qualifi-
cation of the recall measure. 
However, what is not 
clear is the filing responsibility of the official who 
is obligated to file a semi-annual campaign statement 
by July 31, 1979. 
You advise that, under these circumstances, 
the officeholder/candidate should file a Form 430 cover 
and summary page, with no schedules attached, if the 
candidate has not received any contributions or made 
any expenditures during the reporting period. 
Since 
the independent committee is raising in excess of $200, 
ostensibly on behalf of the candidate, you indicated it 
would not be appropriate to file a Form 470. 
Under 
these circumstances, it would be further be inappropriate 
to file a Form 490. 
o 
of 
s 
intended to rely on 
Thank you for your assistance. 
Peter A. 
SACRAMENTO OFF1CE - SUITE 550, 1225 E1GHTH STREET, SACRAMENTO, CA 95814 
• (916) 446-6752 
LOS ANGELES OFF1CE - SU1TE 309,612 SOUTH FLOWER STREET, LOS ANGELES, CA 90017 
• (213) 628 5867

sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign statement filing requirements for a public official subject to a recall when an independent anti-recall committee is raising funds on their behalf?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The officeholder should file a Form 430 cover and summary page with no schedules if they have received no contributions or made no expenditures; Forms 470 and 490 should not be filed when an independent committee is raising funds on behalf of the candidate.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence letter from an attorney to FPPC staff confirming telephonic advice received. It documents FPPC guidance but is written by the requester, not authored by FPPC. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The advice is paraphrased in the body of the letter rather than in a formal response format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the campaign statement filing requirements for a public official subject to a recall when an independent anti-recall committee is raising funds on their behalf?

CONCLUSION: The officeholder should file a Form 430 cover and summary page with no schedules if they have received no contributions or made no expenditures; Forms 470 and 490 should not be filed when an independent committee is raising funds on behalf of the candidate.

Box 807 Sacramento, California Dear Ms. Kutchins: This will confirm our telephone conversation on July 26, 1979 regarding the filing of appropriate campaign statements by a public official who is being recalled. I explained that a recall has been instituted against the public official. A separate committee has been established to oppose the recall effort which is independent of the official being recalled. It is clear that the anti-recall committee, acting for the benefit of the official being recalled, must file its campaign reports 35 days after the qualifi- cation of the recall measure. However, what is not clear is the filing responsibility of the official who is obligated to file a semi-annual campaign statement by July 31, 1979. You advise that, under these circumstances, the officeholder/candidate should file a Form 430 cover and summary page, with no schedules attached, if the candidate has not received any contributions or made any expenditures during the reporting period. embedding.summary: An attorney confirms a telephone conversation with FPPC staff regarding campaign filing requirements for a recalled public official, documenting FPPC's advice that the officeholder should file a Form 430 with no schedules if they have no campaign activity, while an independent anti-recall committee must file separately.
